Special Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Uchiyama (415-563-9300) of Law Offices of Karen Uchiyama for Elizabeth Arnold and Stephen Major, defendants and moving parties / Cathy Mosbrucker (415-398-9880) of Mosbrucker & Foran for David Sierra and Kristen Haviland, plaintiffs and opposing parties

HEARING - Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument advises counsels that its leaning is to deny defendants' special motion to strike for the primary reasons set forth in the opposition. If the court decides that Bullard is to control both parties will be asked for supplemental briefing. If Bullard does not control the court will rely on the pleadings already submitted. Parties are to exchange orders and provide propsed orders to the court. Counsel for defendant submits a proposed order. DEFENDANTs ELIZABETH ARNOLD and STEPHEN MAJOR's Special Motion To Strike Complaint is taken under submission. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, Freddie Reppond - Stenographic Reporter (415-469-8867)
